name: QRadar-0010 - Cryptocurrency Mining Activity Detection
description: Detects unauthorized cryptocurrency mining (cryptojacking) by identifying network connections to known mining pools, Stratum protocol traffic patterns, and DNS queries to mining-related domains. Cryptomining malware consumes resources, increases electricity costs, and often indicates a broader compromise — attackers monetize access to infected systems by deploying miners.

detection.log_source_type: Network Flows (QFlow), DNS Logs, Proxy Logs, Firewall
detection.event_name_filter: Stratum Protocol, Mining Pool Connection, Cryptocurrency DNS Query
detection.aql: SELECT sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort, DomainName, COUNT(*) as connection_count, SUM(bytesTotal) as total_bytes FROM flows WHERE IsInternal(sourceIP) = TRUE AND (destinationPort IN (3333, 4444, 5555, 7777, 8888, 9999, 14444, 14433, 45700) OR DomainName ILIKE '%xmr%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%monero%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%nicehash%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%minexmr%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%moneroocean%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%cryptonight%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%pool.minexmr%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%stratum%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%coinhive%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%cryptoloot%' OR DomainName ILIKE '%webminerpool%') GROUP BY sourceIP, destinationIP, destinationPort, DomainName HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 LAST 30 MINUTES

response.offense_description: An internal host is communicating with known cryptocurrency mining pools or using the Stratum protocol. This indicates unauthorized cryptojacking, which often follows a system compromise.
response.recommended_action: Isolate affected host, perform full malware scan, investigate initial access vector, review for additional indicators of compromise

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rule: net_crypto_mining_pool_connection. Mining pool list should be updated regularly from threat intelligence feeds. Browser-based miners (Coinhive-style) often communicate over WebSocket on port 443 — consider TLS inspection for full coverage. Correlate with high CPU usage events from endpoint monitoring.
author: Security Team
